subject: Geography
title: Denali: North America's Roof
description: One-minute skill: Denali: North America's Roof.

hook.text: Denali rises 6,190 meters in Alaska—the tallest peak in North America.

buildup.text: Measured from base to summit, Denali is taller than Everest. Its base starts near sea level.

discovery.text: Temperatures at the summit can drop to minus 60°C with hurricane-force winds.

twist.text: The mountain was renamed from McKinley to Denali in 2015, honoring its indigenous name.

climax.text: Denali creates its own weather systems, trapping climbers for days in sudden storms.

punchline.text: North America's tallest peak makes its own storms.

quiz.question: What does 'Denali' mean?
options: It is the mountain's indigenous name
It was named after a president
